compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Run /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh (or systemctl start rebar-observability.service) on a healthy box.
2. All sections execute and every metric publishes (trace confirms root_pct etc. reach put-metric-data).
3. The script's last statement is the mirror-out-of-sync guard `[ $oos -gt 0 ] && ...` which evaluates false on a healthy box, and there is no trailing `exit 0` — so the script's exit status is 1.

**Expected:** a run that publishes all metrics exits 0; systemd shows the unit succeeded. **Actual:** exit 1 on every healthy run; journald logs 'Failed with result exit-code' every 5 minutes, the unit sits in failed state (masking real failures and poisoning any future OnFailure/unit-failure monitoring), and install-observability.sh's verification start appears to fail.

Observed 2026-07-10 on the prod box while verifying ac14's root-disk metric (bash -x trace: last line `+ '[' 0 -gt 0 ']'` then exit=1).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] observability.sh exits 0 on a healthy run (e.g. explicit `exit 0` at end, or guard restructured as `if`).
- [ ] A regression test or shellcheck-style check covers the exit status (mirror the test_autodeploy_prune.py stub-harness pattern if practical).
- [ ] systemd unit shows active(exited)/success after a timer run on the box post-deploy.
- [ ] File paths: infra/scripts/observability.sh.
compiled_state.title: observability.sh exits 1 on every healthy run — final statement is a false test with no exit 0
